channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Turkey's soaring costs are creating a 'lost generation' of kids forced to help their families get by
item.description: One-third of Turkey's children live in poverty, with many forced to work to support their struggling families.
item.dc:creator: Robert Badendieck, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: After 20 years, the post-tsunami generation stays vigilant for future disasters
item.description: Qurrata Ayuni, a 28-year-old survivor of the earthquake and tsunami that devastated her hometown two decades ago, has transformed her resilience into purpose.
item.dc:creator: Edna Tarigan, Achmad Ibrahim, And Fadlan Syam, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A Haiti gang attack on the reopening of a main hospital kills 2 reporters and a police officer
item.description: Haiti’s online media association says two reporters were killed and several were wounded in a gang attack on the reopening of Port-au-Prince’s biggest public hospital.
item.dc:creator: Evens Sanon, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Pope Francis kicks off a yearlong Jubilee that will test his stamina and Rome's patience
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — Pope Francis kicked off the 2025 Holy Year on Tuesday, inaugurating a celebration of the Catholic Church that is expected to draw some 32 million pilgrims to Rome in a test of the pope's stamina and the ability of the Eternal City to welcome them.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Toyota to donate $1 million to Trump inauguration
item.description: President-elect Trump’s inauguration is getting $1 million from Toyota Motor North America. A spokesperson for Toyota confirmed the inauguration donation to The Hill on Tuesday. Toyota joins Ford and a number of other companies in pledging significant donations to Trump's inauguration.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Fire alarm scare forces evacuations from Eiffel Tower
item.description: The entire tower was closed temporarily, but access to the first two floors was quickly restored, according to Newsweek.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, NewsNation

item.title: Denmark to boost Greenland defense amid Trump comments
item.description: Denmark announced a major new package to boost the security of Greenland in a move the Danish defense minister called "ironic" because it came just two days after President-elect Trump called for U.S. ownership of the Arctic island.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: France has a new government, again. Politics and crushing debt complicate next steps
item.description: PARIS (AP) — France’s president and prime minister managed to form a new government just in time for the holidays. Now comes the hard part.
item.dc:creator: ANGELA CHARLTON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Baby mammoth preserved for 50,000 years is unveiled in Russia's Siberia
item.description: The 50,000-year-old remains of a baby mammoth uncovered by melting permafrost have been unveiled to the public by researchers in Russia’s Siberia region.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Netanyahu warns Houthis amid calls for Israel to wipe out terror leadership as it did with Nasrallah, Sinwar
item.description: Former Israeli officials discuss the possibility of Jerusalem targeting the Houthi leadership in the same way it took out other terror leaders from Hamas and Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rahm Emanuel rips US defense firms, calls for punishments in final Tokyo missive
item.description: U.S. Ambassador to Japan Rahm Emanuel is calling for defense firms that miss deadlines and increase costs to be forbidden from bidding on new military contracts as a punishment.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: US and UK criticize Pakistani military court convictions of civilian supporters of Imran Khan
item.description: The United States and the United Kingdom have expressed deep concern over the recent handing down of convictions by Pakistani military courts to 25 civilian supporters of former Prime Minister Imran Khan over last year's riots.
item.dc:creator: Munir Ahmed, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: UN investigative team says Syria's new authorities 'very receptive' to probe of Assad war crimes
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The U.N. organization assisting in investigating the most serious crimes in Syria said Monday the country’s new authorities were “very receptive” to its request for cooperation during a just-concluded visit to Damascus, and it is preparing to deploy.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Iran recruiting children to attack Israeli targets across Europe amid setbacks: report
item.description: Iran has been hiring young people in Europe to attack Israeli and Jewish targets in countries across the continent, according to media reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Greenland to Trump: We're not for sale
item.description: Greenland’s prime minister said Monday the autonomous island is not for sale after President-elect Trump said owning Greenland “is an absolute necessity” for the U.S. “Greenland is ours.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Trump could face renewed ISIS threat in Syria as Turkey goes after US ally
item.description: Concerns are mounting that President-elect Trump could face an ISIS resurgence in Syria as Turkey-backed forces hit U.S.-allied SDF troops in a move that could divide the coalitions abilities to fight ISIS.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former Greek royal family expresses 'deep emotion' after regaining citizenship
item.description: ATHENS, Greece (AP) — Members of Greece’s former royal family expressed “deep emotion” Monday at a decision to reinstate their Greek citizenship, ending a decades-old dispute with the country’s government.
item.dc:creator: DEREK GATOPOULOS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Latvia claims to have displayed the world's first Christmas tree in 1510 adorned with artificial roses
item.description: Latvia claims to hold the title of the world's first Christmas tree, as does Estonia. Several sources cite that the first written record of a Christmas tree belongs to Latvia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 2 US Navy pilots shot down over Red Sea in apparent 'friendly fire' incident, US military says
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Two U.S. Navy pilots were shot down Sunday over the Red Sea in an apparent “friendly fire” incident, the U.S military said, marking the most serious incident to threaten troops in over a year of America targeting Yemen's Houthi rebels.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: How China could boost Trump’s interest in Africa
item.description: President Biden made what may be his diplomatic curtain call this month with a long-promised trip to Africa, becoming the first president to visit Angola and first since former President Obama to visit the continent — with President-elect Trump in between.
item.dc:creator: Joanne Haner, The Hill

item.title: What we know about the deadly Christmas market attack in Germany
item.description: MAGDEBURG, Germany (AP) — Four women and a 9-year-old boy were killed and 200 people were injured when a man drove into a Christmas market teeming with holiday shoppers in the German city of Magdeburg — an attack that has left Germans mourning the victims and with a shaken sense of security.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: LIVE Tracker: Santa Claus is en route in his flight across the world to deliver gifts
item.description: When Saint Nick starts his journey on Dec. 24, families can track Santa's sleigh using the NORAD Santa tracker.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Two US Navy pilots shot down over Red Sea in apparent 'friendly fire' incident: US military
item.description: Two U.S. Navy pilots were shot down Sunday over the Red Sea in what appeared to be "friendly fire", but were found alive, the U.S. military said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Knife attack in Croatian school leaves 7-year-old dead, 6 people wounded, police say
item.description: A young male in Croatia's capital killed a 7-year-old girl and wounded a teacher and five other students in a knife attack at Precko Elementary School.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
